title: plantago coronopus subsp. ceratophylla
intro: Plantago coronopus subsp. ceratophylla, also known as buck's horn plantain, is a subspecies of the plantago coronopus plant, which belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) and is a type of flowering plant. The medicinal properties of buck's horn plantain include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of buck's horn plantain include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health conditions. Buck's horn plantain can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
